cases.input: Create Linear issues from our payment processing backlog. We have 1 epic, 3 features, and 10 user stories.
cases.context: Team: Platform Engineering, Project: Q3 Payments, Linear MCP server connected, backlog file ready

cases.input: What context needs to be collected before creating Linear issues from a backlog?
cases.context: Linear MCP, team name required, project name required, cycle optional, team/project must be verified to exist

cases.input: Before creating issues, how do we ensure the required labels exist in Linear for work item types?
cases.context: Linear MCP, list_issue_labels to check existing, create_issue_label for missing: epic, feature, risk, impediment, ci-item

cases.input: How should backlog priority values (1=Must, 2=Should, 3=Could) map to Linear priority levels?
cases.context: Linear priority levels: Urgent, High, Medium, Low, None; backlog priority 1/2/3 scale

cases.input: How do parent-child relationships get established when creating Linear issues from a hierarchical backlog?
cases.context: Epic issues created first capturing IDs, features with parentId set to epic ID, stories with parentId set to feature ID
